title: Lab 8: Rotational Motion — Pre-lab
_provisional: Expected values are a self-consistent placeholder problem (uniform disk M=2.00 kg, R=0.100 m; hanging mass m=0.500 kg falling h=1.00 m; g=9.80 m/s²; part 3: identical disk dropped onto the spinning disk). The pre-lab sheet contains no given values — instructor must confirm or replace these with the real problem's numbers before use.

sections.title: Question (1): Show the relations
sections.instructions: Please show the relations (use as many paper sheets as you need). Your TA will grade your derivations.

sections.title: Question (2): Compute the quantities
sections.instructions: A uniform disk (M = 2.00 kg, R = 0.100 m) is free to rotate about its axle. A string wrapped around its rim supports a hanging mass m = 0.500 kg, released from rest and falling h = 1.00 m (g = 9.80 m/s²). Express answers to 3 sig. figs.

sections.title: Question (3): Compute the quantities
sections.instructions: The disk is now spinning at the angular velocity it reached in Question (2) when an identical disk is dropped onto it and they rotate together. Express answers to 3 sig. figs.

q1_1.label: 1. Relation between the hanging mass's acceleration and the disk's angular acceleration
q1_1.hint: Show your work on paper or summarize it here — graded by your TA

q1_2.label: 2. Relation between torque, moment of inertia, and angular acceleration
q1_2.hint: Show your work on paper or summarize it here — graded by your TA

q2_u.label: Hanging mass's initial potential energy U

q2_kh.label: Hanging mass's final kinetic energy K_h

q2_kd.label: Disk's final rotational kinetic energy K_d
